BUILD/LINT: `npm run build` green — route table confirms all 4 `/projects/[slug]` pages prerender as SSG (● marker: aiklao, typing-race, tiger-kick, jpd-api) plus static `/_not-found`; `npm run lint` clean (0 output). RESPONSIVE: Playwright at 375/768/1280 on all 4 routes — zero horizontal overflow (scrollWidth===innerWidth, 12/12 checks); screenshots of /projects/aiklao (widest content: ASCII architecture diagram in a code fence + 4-col metrics strip) confirm clean layout at all 3 widths, code fence scrolls horizontally inside its own box rather than pushing page width. DARK MODE: dark-only V2 tokens throughout, no light-mode remnants visible in any screenshot; consistent with site-wide dark-only (no toggle, per ADR-0003). CLS/LIGHTHOUSE (mobile emulation): /projects/aiklao full-category run Perf 98 / A11y 100 / BP 100 / SEO 100, CLS 0, 0 console errors. Per-route CLS spot check (perf-only preset) on all 4: aiklao 0, typing-race 0.028, jpd-api 0.012 — all effectively negligible. tiger-kick measured 0.088 (reproducible across 2 runs after clearing stray Chrome processes, same score both times) — still under the ADR-0003 gate (<0.1) but notably non-zero; traced via Lighthouse's layout-shifts audit to the Role/Stack `<dl>` in the case-study header reflowing on JetBrains Mono web-font swap, amplified by Tiger Kick's unusually long `role` frontmatter string (154 chars vs 41-100 on the other 3) causing a different line-wrap pre/post font-load under mobile throttling; does NOT reproduce on an unthrottled browser (Playwright PerformanceObserver measured CLS=0, 0 entries on all 4 routes with real font loading, no throttling) — filed as follow-up bug PF-M2-07 (non-blocking for this card since 0.088 < 0.1 project gate; flagged because it deviates from the card's literal 'no CLS' wording and from M1.5's established CLS-0 baseline). A11Y: axe-core (wcag2a+wcag2aa) on all 4 routes — 0 violations, 0 incomplete; heading order sane on all 4 (single H1 -> sequential H2 section headings, matches template: Overview/My role/Stack/Architecture/Hard problem/Testing/Results/Links/What I learned); skip-to-content link is the first Tab stop everywhere (href=#main-content) with a visible focus ring (box-shadow, teal + dark outline, confirmed via computed style); rehype-slug + rehype-autolink-headings (behavior:'wrap') deep-link anchors are keyboard-reachable in natural document order, accessible name = heading text, not confusing (0 axe violations corroborates); focus-visible rings present on all interactive elements via mdx-components.tsx and page.tsx's shared CTA/anchor classes. LINKS: grep of every rendered href across all 4 routes — zero `#` or placeholder hrefs; only real internal anchors (nav, in-page section links), frontmatter-driven external links (only typing-race has a verified `links.live` -> race.aiklaotrip.com; the other 3 have no `links` key so the header nav correctly renders nothing, per the ARCHITECTURE.md renderer contract), and the constant header/footer GitHub+LinkedIn links. Confirmed every `[NEEDS-VERIFICATION: ...]` marker in MDX body text (aiklao x3, jpd-api x1, tiger-kick x1, typing-race x3) renders as inert plain text in the shipped HTML (verified in both the SSR HTML and the RSC flight payload — no `<a href>` wraps any of them), per the accepted architecture convention. CONTENT FOLLOW-UP (independently confirmed, filed as PF-M2-08, non-blocking for this card): the `typing-race.mdx` internal author note ('this line must not render publicly until resolved', ~line 81) is confirmed rendering as visible plain text inside a real `<li>` in the live page's HTML — this is a content-authoring bug (should have been an MDX comment), not a renderer defect; the renderer is behaving exactly per the accepted convention for these markers. cards.summary: Bug filed by qa-engineer during the PF-M2-06 DoD gate (2026-07-18, live Lighthouse 13.4.0 mobile-emulation runs against a fresh `npm run build && npm run start`, commit 5667844 tree): `/projects/tiger-kick` measures CLS 0.088 (reproducible identically across 2 runs, after clearing stray Chrome processes to rule out measurement noise), noticeably higher than the other 3 case-study routes (aiklao 0, typing-race 0.028, jpd-api 0.012) and a regression from M1.5's established CLS-0 baseline. Lighthouse's `layout-shifts` audit traces the shift to the Role/Stack `<dl>` block in the case-study header (`app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx`), reflowing when the JetBrains Mono web font swaps in; Tiger Kick's frontmatter `role` string is 154 chars (vs 41-100 chars on the other 3 studies), long enough that the fallback-font-vs-webfont line-wrap differs, causing a real (if borderline) shift under Lighthouse's standard mobile-throttling profile. Does NOT reproduce on an unthrottled browser (Playwright PerformanceObserver measured CLS=0 on all 4 routes with real font loading, no throttling) — this is specifically a slow-network/mobile-CPU-throttled font-swap issue. Currently still under the ADR-0003 project gate (CLS < 0.1), so non-blocking for PF-M2-06 and not by itself a Lighthouse category failure, but flagged because it violates PF-M2-06's own literal DoD wording ('no CLS') and because M3's Lighthouse exit-gate run could tip closer to or over 0.1 under real (non-simulated) network/device conditions or if the role text grows.
cards.dod: `/projects/tiger-kick` Lighthouse mobile CLS re-measures at 0 (or a documented, producer-approved rationale for why a small non-zero value is acceptable is recorded); re-verified live by qa-engineer on a fresh production build. Suggested fix directions (frontend-engineer to choose): reserve layout space for the Role/Stack `<dl>` (e.g. min-height) so a font-swap reflow can't change its box height, or use `size-adjust`/`adjustFontFallback` (next/font default) tuning to shrink the fallback-vs-webfont metric mismatch, or cap/clamp long `role` strings' visual line count independent of font-swap timing.

cards.dev_note: REWORK 2026-07-19 (frontend-engineer, after code-reviewer FAIL of commit fdbeea7). The FAIL was correct: the first attempt kept the collapsing negative margin (-my-2.5 = 10px spill / -my-3 = 12px spill) unconditionally, so at wrap/stack points where the flow-slot gap (4-12px) was smaller than 2x the spill, the invisible 44px hit boxes overlapped (overlap = 2*spill - gap). Root fix: the spill is now applied ONLY at breakpoints where each element is provably single-row, and DROPPED (no negative margin) wherever the layout can wrap -- with zero spill each 44px box equals its flow slot, so wrapped rows separated by any row-gap can never overlap (structural guarantee, not arithmetic). Changes vs last attempt: (1) nav-link.tsx -- `-my-2.5` -> `md:-my-2.5` (header is single-row at md+/768px+, wraps only below md); (2) header.tsx brand link -- `-my-2.5` -> `md:-my-2.5`; (3) trust-strip.tsx ITEM_CLASSES -- `-my-2.5` -> `xl:-my-2.5` (strip is single-row only at xl/1280px+, wraps at 375 and 768). footer.tsx and the header GitHub-icon ::after (both already correct per reviewer) were left untouched. Verified by driving real headless Chrome via the DevTools Protocol (zero new deps; Node 24 global WebSocket) and reading actual getBoundingClientRect() hit boxes -- NOT paper arithmetic this time. Harness first reproduced every overlap the reviewer reported on the old build (375 header brand^nav 5.66px / nav-row1^row2 15.65px / nav^github 5.66px; 768+375 trust rows 8px), then confirmed ZERO overlap after the fix across 9 widths (375/640/700/767/768/1024/1100/1279/1280) on BOTH `next dev` and a production `next build && next start`. Every interactive target measures h=44px and w>=44px at 375/768/1280. Trade-off (as reviewer green-lit): on wrapping viewports the affected rows are now 44px tall instead of ~24px (mobile header/trust-strip and the 768px trust-strip are taller) -- this is the intended a11y improvement, real 44px touch targets, not a regression; the primary desktop view (1280px) keeps the exact original tight single-row rhythm because spill is active there. build + lint green. Ready for code-reviewer re-review.
cards.qa_note: QA PASS 2026-07-19 (qa-engineer), commit fea3372, independent re-verification on a fresh production build (npm run build && npm run start), not trusting the prior reports. Drove real headless Chrome (Chrome 150) via raw CDP/WebSocket and read actual getBoundingClientRect() data for header brand, all 4 nav links, header GitHub icon, both footer links, and all 4 trust-strip links. Measured at 8 widths (375/640/767/768/769/1024/1279/1280 -- deliberately including both sides of the md and xl wrap boundaries where the original bug lived): zero pairwise overlap at every width, every target >=24px hard floor, every target =44px tall except the header GitHub icon (visible box stays 36x36 by design). Independently hit-tested the GitHub icon's invisible `::after` expansion via elementFromPoint at its true corners -- confirmed it actually receives clicks (not just paints), but its real expanded box measures ~42x42px, not the full 44x44 the code comment/dev-note describes: the `-inset-1` (4px) is applied relative to the anchor's padding box, and the 1px border eats 2px total off the intended expansion (44 - 2 = 42). This is a minor documentation/comment overstatement, not a DoD failure -- 42px still clears the 24px hard floor by a wide margin and is 2px off the preferred (non-mandatory) 44px target; not filing a bug for it. Keyboard: tabbed through the full home-page order (skip-link -> brand -> nav 01-04 -> GitHub icon -> hero CTAs -> trust-strip x4 -> project card -> footer GitHub -> footer LinkedIn) -- logical top-to-bottom order, no traps, every stop shows the same consistent 2px dark + 2px teal focus ring, no misaligned/oversized rings. Visual regression: screenshotted 375/768/1280 -- 375 header renders as 2 rows (brand+GitHub row, then wrapped nav rows) exactly as documented, taller but not broken/cramped/overlapping; 768 and 1280 keep the tight single-row desktop rhythm. axe-core 4.12.1 run on `/` (real WCAG2a/2aa/21aa/22aa ruleset): 0 violations; 1 unrelated 'incomplete' (color-contrast on an aria-hidden decorative arrow glyph in a project card, outside this card's scope). `npm run build` and `npm run lint` both green; zero console errors/warnings across /, /about, /contact, /resume, /projects at 375 and 1280. Verdict: PASS. Moved review -> done.

cards.summary: Bug filed by qa-engineer during the PF-M3-01 DoD gate (2026-07-18, curl against a fresh `npm run build && npm run start`, commit 55773e2 tree): all 4 case-study routes (`/projects/aiklao`, `/projects/typing-race`, `/projects/tiger-kick`, `/projects/jpd-api`) render with ZERO `og:image`/`og:image:*`/`twitter:image*` meta tags — confirmed by extracting every rendered `<meta property="og:*">`/`<meta name="twitter:*">` tag on all 4 routes, none contain an image key. Root cause: `app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx`'s `generateMetadata` returns its own partial `openGraph: {type, title, description, url}` object (no `images`); Next.js metadata resolution does not deep-merge this against the root `app/layout.tsx` `openGraph` object (which is where the file-convention `app/opengraph-image.tsx` image normally gets attached for the site) — declaring any `openGraph` key at a child segment replaces the whole object, dropping the image. `/`, `/about`, `/contact`, `/resume`, `/projects` (which do NOT declare their own `openGraph`) still get the image fine, confirming the mechanism. Result: sharing any of the 4 case-study links on Twitter/LinkedIn/Facebook/Slack shows no preview image at all. The `app/layout.tsx` comment ('The root opengraph-image.tsx file convention supplies the default OG + Twitter image for every route automatically — no per-page image needed') is incorrect for any route that sets its own `openGraph` object.

cards.summary: Bug filed by qa-engineer during the DoD gate for the uncommitted 'Option A full-bleed media + in-view autoplay' work. The PO's task explicitly required a real keyboard Tab-sequence pass confirming 'every row is a single clean tab stop' on both `/` and `/projects`. On `/projects` this holds (all 4 rows reachable in order, verified). On `/` (home) it does NOT: before any scroll, all 3 `01 -- Featured projects` rows (AiKlao, Typing Race, Tiger Kick) compute `visibility: hidden` (confirmed via `getComputedStyle`) -- this is the pre-existing `<Reveal>` wrapper (`components/motion/reveal.tsx`, not part of this diff, used site-wide) applying GSAP's `autoAlpha` 'from' state (`opacity:0` + `visibility:hidden`) before its ScrollTrigger fires on scroll. `visibility:hidden` elements are excluded from both the default browser tab order and the accessibility tree. A real automated Tab-key sweep (20 successive Tab presses from page load, no scrolling) on `/` confirms this in practice: focus goes skip-strip-item -> skip-strip-item -> ... -> trust-strip's last item -> straight to the FeaturedProjects section's 'All projects ->' footer link, WITHOUT ever landing on any of the 3 project row links. A keyboard-only or screen-reader user tabbing sequentially through the homepage from the top -- without independently scrolling first, which a Tab-only workflow does not otherwise require -- cannot reach any of the 3 featured project links at all. This is a real, reproducible WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) / 2.4.3 (Focus Order) failure, not a new regression from this diff (the same `<Reveal>` wrapper already existed around these rows pre-diff), but it sits exactly on the surface this PR modified and directly fails the explicit 'every row is a single clean tab stop' requirement for the home route. Lighthouse/axe scored Accessibility 100/100 on `/` in this same QA pass and did not catch it -- there is no standard axe rule for 'interactive element is visibility:hidden pending a scroll-triggered animation', so this required a live Tab-sequence simulation to surface, not a static a11y scan.
cards.dod: A real automated Tab-key sweep from page load on `/`, with no prior scrolling, reaches all 3 `01 -- Featured projects` row links (AiKlao, Typing Race, Tiger Kick) in order, each with a visible focus ring, before reaching the 'All projects ->' footer link -- i.e. the rows are keyboard-focusable (not `visibility:hidden`) from first paint, even though their entry-animation may still play visually on scroll. Likely fix: change `<Reveal>`'s GSAP tween to animate `opacity`/`autoAlpha`-equivalent without a `visibility` toggle for elements that contain interactive children (e.g. use `opacity` alone, or gate `autoAlpha`'s visibility behavior off), OR make the affected `<ProjectMediaRow>` instances on the home route bypass `<Reveal>`'s pre-hidden state specifically. Confirm no regression to the visual entry-animation itself under normal mouse/scroll use, and that `/about`/`/contact`/etc. (other `<Reveal>` consumers) are considered if the fix is component-wide, since this is pre-existing shared-component behavior, not scoped to project rows alone. qa-engineer re-runs the same live Tab-sequence test before this card can move to done.

Updated the JSDoc with the ruling's stated guarantee line. VERIFIED live (Playwright, prod build, 1280x800, scripted Tab-key sweep from page load with NO prior scroll; wrap-around detected via the skip link re-focus so only the genuine FIRST pass is credited -- auto-scroll from tabbing to the footer is not). Controlled before/after: BEFORE (reverted to autoAlpha, rebuilt) -- 3 featured rows all compute `visibility:hidden` at load; first-pass tab sequence = skip-link, logo, Projects, About, Resume, Contact, GitHub, trust-strip links, footer GitHub/LinkedIn, and NEVER lands on any /projects/aiklao|typing-race|tiger-kick nor the CTA Contact button (reproduces QA's exact finding). AFTER (fix) -- all 3 rows `visibility:visible opacity:1` at load; first-pass Tab reaches /projects/aiklao -> /projects/typing-race -> /projects/tiger-kick IN ORDER, each with a focus-visible ring (box-shadow present) and visibility:visible, THEN 'All projects ->' (/projects), THEN the CTA Contact button (confirmed inside section[aria-labelledby=closing-cta-heading], not the header /contact link). Every DoD verification point confirmed: (a) SSR/no-JS -- raw curl of / has ZERO `opacity:0`/`visibility:hidden` in server HTML and all 3 row links present (JSDoc guarantee holds). (b) Reduced motion -- with reducedMotion:reduce the tween never runs, rows are server-visible (visibility:visible opacity:1) and tab-reachable immediately; identical PASS. (c) skills.tsx (stagger mode) no regression -- 4 group children go [0,0,0,0] at load (below fold, visibility:visible) -> [0.98,0.87,0.58,0.04] mid-stagger cascade on scroll-in -> [1,1,1,1] settled; the deferred scrollTrigger path also verified on the below-fold CTA (opacity 0 at load -> 1 after scroll), so the entry animation itself still works. (d) CLS = 0 on / (PerformanceObserver, both motion modes) -- opacity/transform-only, no layout effect. (e) zero console errors both modes. `npm run build` + `npm run lint` green. Scoped to reveal.tsx (the 3 homepage consumers featured-projects/skills/cta all benefit; /projects WorkRow does not use Reveal, unaffected). Awaiting code-reviewer -> qa-engineer re-run of the live Tab-sequence test.

cards.summary: Structural risk flagged by code-reviewer and agreed by qa-engineer during the LinkedIn URL DoD gate: `LINKEDIN_URL` and `GITHUB_URL` each exist as THREE independent copies in the codebase — one exported const in `lib/site.ts` (feeds homepage JSON-LD `Person.sameAs`) and two separate local consts re-declared with the same literal value in `app/contact/page.tsx` and `components/sections/footer.tsx` — bound together only by a prose comment, not by import. This diff's own DoD gate had to hand-verify all three copies individually to catch a possible missed one; a future edit to only 1-2 of the 3 copies would ship silently (e.g. correct JSON-LD but a dead link in the global footer on every page, or vice versa), and nothing in the build/lint/type pipeline would catch the mismatch since all three are independently valid string literals.
cards.dod: architect decides and documents (ADR or DESIGN_SYSTEM.md note) a consolidation approach so `LINKEDIN_URL`/`GITHUB_URL` have exactly one source of truth (most likely: `app/contact/page.tsx` and `components/sections/footer.tsx` import the already-exported consts from `lib/site.ts` instead of re-declaring local copies), confirms no import-cycle / server-vs-client-component boundary issue with doing so, then hands off implementation to frontend-engineer as a follow-up card. Not a fix-now item — this is a structural change requiring architect approval per CLAUDE.md before any code moves.
